prompt: A Japanese Edo-period ukiyo-e woodblock print in the style of Katsushika Hokusai. A lone traveler descends a steep mountain path, each step carefully chosen downward toward the valley below. The mountain has multiple possible paths but the figure always moves toward the lowest visible point. The composition uses flat unmodulated areas of color — deep indigo, ochre, and cream. Strong diagonal compositional lines lead the eye downward. Visible woodgrain texture throughout. Bold black outlines with no photographic shading. Stylized clouds in the upper portion. The valley floor glows faintly at the bottom suggesting the destination. No text, no labels, no modern elements. Authentic ukiyo-e visual grammar only.
tradition_approach: Targeted Hokusai compositional diagonals, flat unmodulated color areas in indigo and ochre, bold black outlines, visible woodgrain texture, and stark figure-ground contrast — all authentic ukiyo-e grammar elements.
